metadata.name: LANforge CLI Commands
metadata.description: Model Context Protocol dataset for LANforge Command Line Interface (CLI) commands. LANforge is a network testing and emulation platform by Candela Technologies.

tools.name: adb
tools.description: adb is used to control Android devices connected to LANforge systems via USB and/or IP network.

adb_id.description: Android device identifier, use NA if it should not be used/specified.

key.description:
Key to be used in response messages, NA for generic keyed message.
Key should not have - or spaces or other non-alphanumeric characters in it.

metadata.syntax: adb  shelf resource adb_id key adb_cmd

tools.name: adb_bt
tools.description: bluetooth keyboard emulation is used to control ADB devices connected to LANforge systems via USB. Either raw keystrokes or specific actions can be passed in. Raw keystrokes example: [... {adb_id} ctrl h ctrl f s e t t...] Keystroke cmd example: [... {adb_id} __apply_cfg mgr_ip: 192.168.0.50 resource: 20]

adb_id.description: Android device identifier, use NA if it should not be used/specified.

tools.name: adb_timeout
tools.description: adb is used to control Android devices connected to LANforge systems via USB and/or IP network.

adb_id.description: Android device identifier, use NA if it should not be used/specified.

key.description:
Key to be used in response messages, NA for generic keyed message.
Key should not have - or spaces or other non-alphanumeric characters in it.

max_dur.description: Num of milliseconds to let this command run before killing it. 0 indicates no timeout.

metadata.syntax: adb_timeout  shelf resource adb_id key max_dur adb_cmd

tools.name: adb_gui
tools.description: Utilize the MonkeyRemote or scrcpy project to provide an interactive UI for Android devices via the adb protocol. If you do not specify the DISPLAY , LANforge will attempt to guess it based on your connecting IP address. For PCs, you can use the exceed program from Hummingbird software. To enable X access on Unix/Linux, run this command: This can open your machine to security threats, so read up on xhost before you run this command on a mission critical machine not protected by a good firewall! If using scrcpy, screen-size can be > 1.0. 1.0 means '800' screen width. If using MonkeyRemote, then value is percentage of default size. Flags are defined as follows. You can enter the value in HEX if you prefix it with 0x .

display.description: The DISPLAY option, for example: 192.168.1.5:0.0.  Will guess if left blank.

screen_size_prcnt.description: 0.1 to 1.0, screen size percentage for the Android display.

flags.description: See flags defined above.

max_size.description: Limit both the width and height of the video to value. (scrcpy only).  0 (Unlocked )is default.

fps.description: Limit the FPS of the video. (scrcpy only). 20 is default, 0 is unlocked.

metadata.syntax: adb_gui  shelf resource adb_id display screen_size_prcnt flags max_size fps

flags.description: See above

flags_mask.description: Which bits in the flags value to apply.

realm.description: Realm this Interop device should use.  Default is server's realm.

tools.name: add_arm_endp
tools.description: Add an Armageddon endpoint. Armageddon endpoints are kernel accelerated, and often run many times faster than regular LANforge endpoints, especially for smaller packets. The feature set is optimized for quickly generating lots of packets from different source and destination addresses (mac, IP, ip-port, etc).

pkt_sz.description: Minimum packet size, including all Ethernet headers.

mx_pkt_sz.description: Maximum packet size, including all Ethernet headers.

cpu_id.description: Preferred CPU ID on which this endpoint should run.

tos.description: The Type of Service, can be HEX.  See set_endp_tos for details.

metadata.syntax: add_arm_endp  alias shelf resource port type pps pkt_sz mx_pkt_sz cpu_id tos

tools.name: add_cx
tools.description: Add a cross-connect to a test-manager. The endpoints must have already been created.

alias.description: Name of the Cross Connect to create.

test_mgr.description: Name of test-manager to create the CX on. [W]

tools.name: add_cd
tools.description: Add a Collision Domain (CD). A CD is a group of WanLinks and/or Virtual-Routers that are considered to be in the same collision domain. For instance, when emulating clients talking to an AP, all of the WanLinks associated with this emulated AP should be in the same Collision Domain. All WanLinks or Virtual Routers in a CD must be on the same Resource (machine). Currently only the 'WIFI' type is supported unless you have the third-party WISER module loaded (contact your sales rep for info.) The WIFI emulation counts bandwidth when it is transmitted or received (ie, it emulates stations ↔ AP behaviour.) The WISER emulation emulates special military waveforms. An Ethernet Hub emulation is planned for future releases. Flags are defined as follows. The state field over-rides the running flag if state is not NA . You can enter the value in HEX if you prefix it with 0x .

type.description: CD Type:  WIFI, WISER_SURFACE, WISER_SURFACE_AIR, WISER_AIR_AIR, WISER_NCW

bps.description: Maximum speed at which this collision domain can run.

state.description: RUNNING or STOPPED (default is RUNNING).  Use this to start/stop.

flags.description: See above.  Leave blank or use 'NA' for no default values.

metadata.syntax: add_cd  shelf resource alias type bps report_timer state flags

tools.name: add_cd_endp
tools.description: Add an Endpoint to a Collision Domain. The endpoint must be a WanLink Endpoint. If the endpoint is currently in another Collision Domain, it will be migrated to the new one safely.

tools.name: add_cd_vr
tools.description: Add a Virtual Router to a Collision Domain. If the VR is currently in another Collision Domain, it will be migrated to the new one safely.

tools.name: add_chamber
tools.description: Add/Modify a Chamber entry. A chamber may have up to 4 LANforge resources and up to 4 DUTs. It may also have up to 16 RF Connections defined, including Connections with attenuation configured. chamber_flags : Chamber Type : Turntable Type : The resource-id only needs to be set in case there is a serial connection to the chamber turntable or other management control device. For TCP-IP connections, the manager process can connect directly. See add_chamber_cx to configure connection objects.

name.description: Name of Chamber, unique identifier.

flags.description: Flag field for Chamber, see above.

isolation.description: Estimated isolation in db for this chamber.

chamber_type.description: Chamber type, see above.  Use 1 for Medium if uncertain.

dut_name1.description: Name of first DUT in this chamber or NA

dut_name2.description: Name of second DUT in this chamber or NA

dut_name3.description: Name of third DUT in this chamber or NA

dut_name4.description: Name of fourth DUT in this chamber or NA

lanforge1.description: EID of first LANforge Resource in this chamber or NA

lanforge2.description: EID of second LANforge Resource in this chamber or NA

lanforge3.description: EID of third LANforge Resource in this chamber or NA

lanforge4.description: EID of fourth LANforge Resource in this chamber or NA

flags_mask.description: Mask of what flags to pay attention to, or NA for all.

X.description: X coordinate to be used when drawn in the LANforge-GUI.

Y.description: Y coordinate to be used when drawn in the LANforge-GUI.

width.description: Width to be used when drawn in the LANforge-GUI.

height.description: Height to be used when drawn in the LANforge-GUI.

resource.description: LANforge Resource ID for controlling turn-table via serial protocol.

sma_count.description: Number of SMA connectors on this chamber, default is 16.

climate_controller_type.description: Type of climate controller for this chamber.

metadata.syntax: add_chamber  name flags isolation chamber_type dut_name1 dut_name2 dut_name3 dut_name4 lanforge1 lanforge2 lanforge3 lanforge4 flags_mask X Y width height resource turntable_type sma_count climate_controller_type

tools.name: add_chamber_cx
tools.description: Add/Modify a Chamber connection. A chamber may have up to 32 connections defined. chamber_cx_flags :

name.description: Name of Chamber, unique identifier.

connection_idx.description: Connection index, currently up to 32 connections supported (0-31)

internal.description: Internal (1) or not (0):  Internal connections are no longer supported.

flags.description: Flag field for Chamber Connection, see above.

a_id.description: EidAntenna in string format for A side connection.

b_id.description: EidAntenna in string format for B side connection.

atten_id.description: EID for the Attenuator module if one is inline on this connection.

flags_mask.description: Mask of what flags to pay attention to, or NA for all.

min_atten.description: Specify minimum attenuation in 10ths of a db.  Distance logic will not set atten below this.

zrssi2.description: Specify 2.4Ghz zero-attenuation RSSI in 10ths of a db.  Distance logic will consider this in its calculations.

zrssi5.description: Specify 5Ghz zero-attenuation RSSI in 10ths of a db.  Distance logic will consider this in its calculations.

metadata.syntax: add_chamber_cx  name connection_idx internal flags a_id b_id atten_id flags_mask min_atten zrssi2 zrssi5

tools.name: add_chamber_path
tools.description: This text (x,y,ticks triples) will be added to the end of the specified path. The text must be entered one line at a time, primarily due to CLI parsing limitations. X and Y are coordinates, with 0,0 being top-left. Ticks are units of time that the chamber object should stay in the specified location before moving to the next waypoint. When re-playing a path, the ticks will be converted into units of time based on the specified replay speed. Setting the path content to [BLANK] will delete it. You can also use the 'rm_chamber_path' command to delete one or all paths.

tools.name: add_file_endp
tools.description: Add a File endpoint to the LANforge Manager. This endpoint can then be used to read and/or write data from/to the file system. This is most interesting when the file system in question is some sort of network file system like NFS or iSCSI. If the endpoint already exists, then this command may be used to update the values. This defaults to 4096 read/write sizes, but you can change that with the set_fe_info command. Payload Pattern : fio_flags : File Endpoint type :

directory.description: The directory to read/write in.  Absolute path suggested.

prefix.description: The prefix of the file(s) to read/write.

mount_options.description: Optional mount options, passed to the mount command.  'NONE' clears.

fio_flags.description: File-IO flags, see above for details.

mount_dir.description: Directory to mount/unmount (if blank, will use 'directory').

retry_timer.description: Number of miliseconds to retry errored IO calls before giving up.

metadata.syntax: add_file_endp  alias shelf resource port type min_read_rate max_read_rate min_write_rate max_write_rate payload_pattern directory prefix server_mount mount_options fio_flags mount_dir volume retry_timer

tools.name: add_gen_endp
tools.description: Add a Generic endpoint to the LANforge Manager. This endpoint will cause an external program to be run, and the results will be sent back to the LANforge system. Due to parsing constraints, you can only use certain programs, but if LANforge does not support a program you want to use, please request the feature from Candela Technologies. Set the actual command to be executed command with set_gen_cmd

subtype.description: Generic Endpoint Subtype: gen_none, gen_zoom, gen_teams, gen_meet

gen_flags.description: Flags for configuring a generic endpoint.

group.description: Group ID for {gen_zoom, gen_teams, gen_meet} endpoints  [D:NA]

metadata.syntax: add_gen_endp  alias shelf resource port type subtype gen_flags group

tools.name: add_l4_endp
tools.description: Add a Layer 4-7 (HTTP, FTP, TELNET, ...) endpoint to the LANforge Manager. This endpoint can then be used to handle URL(s). If the endpoint already exists, then this command may be used to update the values. If you do not wish to change certain fields from the current value, use NA for the value of these fields. When entering a URL, use this syntax: You need to single quote the whole value. Example download: Example upload: If you want to upload data, use the ftp protocol. If you want to emulate HTTP form posts, please use a Generic Endpoint. Downloaded files are typically /dev/null on Linux and NUL on Windows to save space. Multiple Layer 4 endpoints writing to the same file will probably create a corrupt output. Relative file names will be place in either /home/lanforge on Linux or %LOCALAPPDATA%\LANforge-GUI_{ver} on Windows. Note that Windows can make folders under %LOCALAPPDATA% read only so you might want always write your destination files under %TEMP% . LANforge will not expand environmental variables in file names, so instead of writing $TEMP on Linux or %TEMP%\{name} in Windows you would need to write /tmp/{name} or C:\Users\jreynolds\AppData\Local\Temp\{name} . URL Protocols can be those that curl supports. Here are common examples: http http:// or https://user:password@host/file

proxy_port.description: HTTP Proxy port if you are using a proxy.

timeout.description: How long to wait for a connection, in milliseconds

URL.description: The URL, see syntax above.  Can also be a local file.

proxy_server.description: The name of our proxy server if using one.

user_agent.description: User-Agent string.  Leave blank for default.  Also SMTP-TO: <a@b.com><c@d.com>...<q@x.com>

dns_cache_timeout.description: In seconds, how long to cache DNS lookups.  0 means no caching at all.

max_speed.description: In bits-per-second, can rate limit upload or download speed of the URL contents. 0 means infinite.

ip_addr.description: Local IP address, for binding to specific secondary IP.

quiesce_after.description: Quiesce test after this many URLs have been processed.

quiesce_after_sec.description: Quiesce test after this many seconds have elapsed.

flavor.description: Type of the application to run. See above.

metadata.syntax: add_l4_endp  alias shelf resource port type proxy_port timeout url_rate URL proxy_server proxy_userpwd ssl_cert_fname user_agent proxy_auth_type http_auth_type dns_cache_timeout max_speed block_size smtp_from ip_addr quiesce_after quiesce_after_sec flavor

tools.name: add_channel_group
tools.description: Add a grouping of DS0 channels to be used by PPP connections. Supported formats for the channels entry include: Channel types (for Digium) are described here: These are not currently supported:

span_num.description: The span number.  First span is 1, second is 2...

channels.description: List of channels to add to this group.

type.description: The channel-type.  Use 'clear' for PPP links.

MTU.description: MTU (and MRU) for this channel group.  Must be a multiple of the number of channels if configuring a T1 WanLink.

idle_flag.description: Idle flag (byte) for this channel group, for instance: 0x7e

metadata.syntax: add_channel_group  alias shelf resource span_num channels type MTU idle_flag

tools.name: add_ppp_link
tools.description: Add a PPP interface connection. Currently we only support PPP over channel-groups on T1 interfaces. Some of the arguments below are passed directly to the pppd process which negotiates and otherwise creates the ppp interface. You may want to read the man page for pppd for more in-depth discussion of the features. channel_groups selects the hardware resources that the PPP link will use. For Multi-Link PPP, you can select multiple Channel-Groups, otherwise select a single one. If you are entering multiple groups, surround all groups with single quotes, like: 'cg1 cg2 cg3' mlppp_descriptor should start with ' magic: ' and have some ascii-hex trailing it. For instance: magic:00:11:22:33:44 You can use 'NA' if you are not using Multi-Link PPP. If you need to pass extra arguments to the pppd software, you can add those arguments to the 'extra_args' value. Be sure to surround the input with single quotes so it is parsed correctly by LANforge.

unit.description: Unit number for the PPP link. ie, the 7 in ppp7.

src_ip.description: Source IP address for this PPP connection.

dst_ip.description: Destination IP address for this PPP connection.

channel_groups.description: List of channel groups, see above.

debug.description: YES for debug, otherwise debugging for the ppp connection is off.

auth.description: YES if you want to authenticate.  Default is NO.

persist.description: YES if you want to persist the connection.  This is suggested.

lcp_echo_failure.description: LCP echo failures before we determine links is dead, suggest 5.

holdoff.description: Seconds between attempt to bring link back up if it dies, suggest 1.

mlppp_descriptor.description: A unique key for use with multi-link PPP connections.

extra_args.description: Extra arguments to be passed directly to the pppd server.

transport_type.description: What sort of transport this ppp link uses.

pppoe_transport_port.description: Port number (or name) for underlying PPPoE transport.

tty_transport_device.description: TTY device for PPP links associated with TTYs.

run_time_min_ms.description: Minimum uptime (ms) for PPP link during an experiment, or 0 for the link to be always up.

run_time_max_ms.description: Maximum uptime (ms) for PPP link during an experiment, or 0 for the link to be always up.

down_time_min_ms.description: Minimum length of downtime (ms) for PPP link between runs, or 0 for the link to be always up.

down_time_max_ms.description: Maximum length of downtime (ms) for PPP link between runs, or 0 for the link to be always up.

metadata.syntax: add_ppp_link  shelf resource unit src_ip dst_ip channel_groups debug auth persist lcp_echo_interval lcp_echo_failure holdoff mlppp_descriptor extra_args transport_type pppoe_transport_port tty_transport_device run_time_min_ms run_time_max_ms down_time_min_ms down_time_max_ms

tools.name: add_profile
tools.description: Add LANforge device profile. This can give a high level description of how the LANforge system should act. The profile can then be selected in higher-level test cases to auto-generate lower level configuration. Wifi_Mode profile_type Profile Flags: For mac-address pattern, release 5.4.1 and higher also supports sub-byte randomizations. For instance, this will randomize just the low 4 bits of the second octet: xx:xx:xx:xx:*4:xx See Also: add_traffic_profile

name.description: Profile Name.

freq.description: WiFi frequency to be used, 0 means default.

ssid.description: WiFi SSID to be used, [BLANK] means any.

passwd.description: WiFi Password to be used (AP Mode), [BLANK] means no password.

profile_flags.description: Flags for this profile, see above.

mac_pattern.description: Optional MAC-Address pattern, for instance: xx:xx:xx:*:*:xx

alias_prefix.description: Port alias prefix, aka hostname prefix.

txpower.description: WiFi Radio requested txpower.  -1 means default.

metadata.syntax: add_profile  name profile_type wifi_mode antenna instance_count freq ssid passwd profile_flags flags_mask mac_pattern bandwidth eap_id alias_prefix vid txpower

tools.name: add_profile_notes

The profile can then be selected in higher-level test cases to auto-generate lower level configuration. Type Traffic Profile Flags: See Also: add_profile

name.description: Profile Name.

traffic_profile_flags.description: Flags for this profile, none defined at this point.

metadata.syntax: add_traffic_profile  name type min_speed max_speed min_pdu max_pdu tos instance_count traffic_profile_flags traffic_profile_flags_mask min_speed max_speed

tools.name: add_traffic_profile_notes
tools.description: This text will be added to the end of the notes field for Profiles. The text must be entered one line at a time, primarily due to CLI parsing limitations.

tools.name: add_text_blob

tools.name: add_t1_span
tools.description: Add a T1/E1 SPAN to the LANforge Manager. You will have to actually have T1/E1 hardware in the system before this is a useful thing to do. You will then be able to create channel-groups and PPP links. For the first_channel , the setting will depend on the T1/E1 port you wish to use. The first T1/E1 resource will have the first_channel of 1, the second at 25, the third at 49, etc. Build-out: PPP Link Types : Framing NOTE: d4 is also known as 'sf' or 'superframe'.

span_num.description: The span number.  First span is 1, second is 2...

first_channel.description: The first DS0 channel for this span.

timing.description: Timing:  0 == do not use, 1 == primary, 2 == secondary..

framing.description: Framing:  T1: esf or d4.  E1: cas or ccs.

coding.description: Coding:  T1: ami or b8zs.  E1: ami or hdb3

pci_bus.description: PCI Bus number, needed for Sangoma resources.

pci_slot.description: PCI slot number, needed for Sangoma resources.

CPU_ID.description: CPU identifier (A, B, etc) for multiport Sangoma resources.

MTU.description: MTU for this span (used by in-band management, if at all).

metadata.syntax: add_t1_span  shelf resource type span_num first_channel timing buildout framing coding pci_bus pci_slot CPU_ID MTU

tools.name: add_voip_endp
tools.description: Add a VOIP (Voice over IP) to the LANforge Manager. If the endpoint already exists, then this command may be used to update the values. If the sip_gateway is 'AUTO', then the management IP for that particular machine will be used.

sip_gateway.description: SIP Gateway/Proxy Name, this is who to register with, or AUTO

tx_sound_file.description: File name containing the sound sample we will be playing.

rx_sound_file.description: File name to save received PCM data to.  Will be in WAV format, or AUTO

VAD_timer.description: How much silence (milliseconds) before VAD is enabled.

VAD_max_timer.description: How often should we force a packet, even if VAD is on.

gateway_port.description: IP Port for SIP gateway (defaults to 5060).

display_name.description: User-Name to be displayed.  Use AUTO to display phone number. BT Identifier for Mobile.

proxy_passwd.description: Password to be used when registering with proxy/gateway.

peer_phone_num.description: Use AUTO to use phone number of peer endpoint, otherwise specify a number:  user[@host[:port]]

auth_user_name.description: Use this field for authentication user name.  AUTO or blank mean use phone number.

ip_addr.description: Use this IP for local IP address.  Useful when there are multiple IPs on a port.

mobile_bt_mac.description: Mobile Bluetooth MAC address in xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx format.

metadata.syntax: add_voip_endp  alias shelf resource port phone_num rtp_port sip_gateway tx_sound_file rx_sound_file VAD_timer VAD_max_timer gateway_port display_name proxy_passwd peer_phone_num auth_user_name ip_addr mobile_bt_mac

tools.name: add_vrcx
tools.description: Add or modify a Virtual Router Connection Endpoint. Virtual Router Connection Endpoints are used to logically connect two Virtual Routers with an emulated network link. Typically, 2 pairs of redirect virtual interfaces are bridged by a WanLink (which provides the network emulation.) The 'A' port in each pair of redirect devices is associated with one virtual router and has and IP address. Both endpoints should have the IP on the same subnet. The WanLink bridges the two 'B' sides of the redirect device pair. A pair of Connection Endpoint objects are required, with reversed values in their port configuration to make a connection. Flags can be entered in HEX if preceded by 0x. Add flags together to get desired options. Must use apply_vr_cfg for changes to take effect.

vr_name.description: Virtual Router this endpoint belongs to.  Use 'FREE_LIST' to add a stand-alone endpoint. [R][D:FREE_LIST]

local_dev.description: Name of port A, the local network device pair.

local_dev_b.description: Name of port B for the local redirect device pair.

remote_dev_b.description: Name of port B for the remote network device.

wanlink.description: The name of the WanLink that connects the two B ports.

X.description: X coordinate to be used when drawn in the LANforge-GUI.

Y.description: Y coordinate to be used when drawn in the LANforge-GUI.

width.description: Width to be used when drawn in the LANforge-GUI.

height.description: Height to be used when drawn in the LANforge-GUI.

flags.description: Flags, specify if subnets 0-7 are in use, see above for others.

subnets.description: Subnets associated with this link, format:  1.1.1.1/24,1.1.2.1/16...

nexthop.description: The next-hop to use when routing packets out this interface.

dhcp_min.description: Minimum IP address range to serve.

dhcp_max.description: Minimum IP address range to serve.

interface_cost.description: If using OSPF, this sets the cost for this link (1-65535).

ospf_area.description: If using OSPF, this sets the OSPF area for this interface.  Default is 0.0.0.0.

rip_metric.description: If using RIP, this determines the RIP metric (cost), (1-15, 15 is infinite).

vrrp_ip.description: VRRP IPv4 address..ignored if not flagged for VRRP.

vrrp_ip_prefix.description: Number of bits in subnet mask, ie 24 for 255.255.255.0

vrrp_id.description: VRRP id, must be unique in this virtual router (1-255)

vrrp_priority.description: VRRP Priority (1-255, higher is more priority.)

vrrp_interval.description: VRRP broadcast message interval, in seconds (1-255)

metadata.syntax: add_vrcx  shelf resource vr_name local_dev local_dev_b remote_dev remote_dev_b wanlink X Y width height flags subnets nexthop dhcp_lease_time dhcp_dns dhcp_min dhcp_max dhcp_domain interface_cost ospf_area rip_metric vrrp_ip vrrp_ip_prefix vrrp_id vrrp_priority vrrp_interval dhcp_dns6 dhcp_min6 dhcp_max6

tools.name: add_endp
tools.description: Add an endpoint to the LANforge Manager. The endpoint may then be added to a cross-connect. If the endpoint already exists, then this command may be used to update the values. Note that you can leave everything after 'port' off the command, and default values will be used. If you are configuring a TCP connection to make many connections, then use 0 (zero) for the IP Port so that the OS can choose a new one for each connection. Payload_pattern can be: Endpoint Types can be of these types:

ip_port.description:
IP Port: IP port for layer three endpoints. Use -1 to let the LANforge server
                 automatically configure the ip_port. Layer 2 endpoints will ignore
                 this argument.  Use 0 for 'ANY', and let the OS choose.

is_rate_bursty.description: Yes means bursty, anything else means NO.

min_rate.description: Minimum transmit rate (bps), or only rate if not bursty.

max_rate.description: Maximum transmit rate (bps), used if in bursty mode.

is_pkt_sz_random.description: Yes means use random sized packets, anything else means NO.

min_pkt.description: Minimum packet size, including all headers.  -1 means AUTO (5.3.2+)

max_pkt.description: Maximum packet size, including all headers.  0 means 'same', -1 means AUTO (5.3.2+)

use_checksum.description: Yes means checksum the payload, anything else means NO.

ttl.description: Time-to-live, used by UDP Multicast Endpoints only.

send_bad_crc_per_million.description: If NIC supports it, will randomly send X per million packets with bad ethernet Frame Check Sum.

multi_conn.description: If > 0, will create separate process with this many connections per endpoint.  See AUTO_HELPER flag

metadata.syntax: add_endp  alias shelf resource port type ip_port is_rate_bursty min_rate max_rate is_pkt_sz_random min_pkt max_pkt payload_pattern use_checksum ttl send_bad_crc_per_million multi_conn

event_id.description: Numeric ID for the event to modify, or 'new' if creating a new one. [W]

details.description: Event text description.  Cannot include double-quote characters.

name.description: Event entity name.

tools.name: add_mvlan
tools.description: Add a MAC based VLAN. This command requires that the designated machine support the macvlan kernel module. A MAC-VLAN interface is a light-weight virtual interface that is made unique by its MAC address. Do not add two MAC vlans with the same MAC to the same interface. In most cases, you do not want to duplicate a MAC at all! After creating the MAC-VLAN interface, you will need to configure its IP and other information. If you wish to create a MAC VLAN with a specific name, specify the index as well. If not specified, one will be automatically selected for you. For mac-address pattern, release 5.4.1 and higher also supports sub-byte randomizations. For instance, this will randomize just the low 4 bits of the second octet: xx:xx:xx:xx:*4:xx

port.description: Port number of an existing Ethernet interface.

old_name.description: The temporary name, used for configuring un-discovered hardware.

report_timer.description: Report timer for this port, leave blank or use NA for defaults.

flags.description: 0x1:  Create admin-down.

metadata.syntax: add_mvlan  shelf resource port MAC index old_name report_timer flags

tools.name: add_rdd
tools.description: Add a Redirect-Device. This command requires that the designated machine support the redirdev kernel module. Redirect-Devices act like a pair of physical Ethernet interfaces connected externally by a loop-back cable, and are useful for creating virtual networks. Currently, the main reason to do this is to run LANforge ICE on a single interface in conjunction with routing. The basic idea is to create a pair of redirect devices. Give one an IP address that you want the local machine to have. The other redirect interface in the pair will not have an IP address and will be bridged by LANforge ICE (WanLink) to the real Ethernet interface, which also will not have an IP address. It is possible to add 802.1Q and MAC-VLANs on top of redirect devices as well. To create an redirect-device pair, run this command twice, for example:

port.description: Name of the Redirect Device to create.

peer_ifname.description: The peer (other) RedirectDevice in this pair.

report_timer.description: Report timer for this port, leave blank or use NA for defaults.

metadata.syntax: add_rdd  shelf resource port peer_ifname report_timer

If you are not sure what this is for, then it is not for you! freq_24 : 16-bit number to specify 2.4Ghz channels to use. OR the values together to choose a list of available channels, freq_5 : See this page for cooresponding frequencies: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_WLAN_channels

tools.name: add_sta
tools.description: Add a WIFI Virtual Station (Virtual STA) interface. This command requires that the designated machine support LANforge driver for the Atheros brand WIFI NICs. A Virtual STA interface is a virtual interface that acts like a real wireless client. After creating the Virtual STA interface, you will need to configure its IP and other information. NA can be used for any values that you do not wish to modify. Flags are currently defined as: To set any value to the default (or un-set), use DEFAULT . You may have to reboot the system to have the defaults take affect. Rate configuration: Groups: 802.11b 802.11/a/g 802.11/a/b/g 1 Stream /n 2 Streams /n 3 Streams /n v-1 Stream /AC v-2 Streams /AC v-3 Streams /AC Mode For mac-address pattern, release 5.4.1 and higher also supports sub-byte randomizations. For instance, this will randomize just the low 4 bits of the second octet: xx:xx:xx:xx:*4:xx

radio.description: Name of the physical radio interface, for example: wiphy0

sta_name.description: Name for this Virtual STA, for example:  sta0

key.description: Encryption key (WEP, WPA, WPA2, WPA3, etc) for this Virtual STA.  Prepend with 0x for ascii-hex input.

flags_mask.description: If set, only these flags will be considered.

ieee80211w.description: Management Frame Protection:  0:  disabled, 1: optional, 2: Required.

rsn_override.description: Check for RSN Override IEs: 0: disabled, 1: Enable if supported, 2: Force enabled.

tools.name: add_wanpath
tools.description: Add a WanPath personality to a WanLink. The WanPath is like a virtual WanLink between a source and destination IP or IP range. For instance, if you want communications between server A and client C to be different from communications between server B and client C, then you can set up two WanPaths to specify that behaviour. If the specified WanPath already exists, this command can be used to modify the existing values

wanlink.description: Name of WanLink to which we are adding this WanPath.

speed.description: The maximum speed this WanLink will accept (bps).

latency.description: The base latency added to all packets, in milliseconds (or add 'us' suffix for microseconds)

max_jitter.description: The maximum jitter, in milliseconds (or add 'us' suffix for microseconds)

extra_buffer.description: The extra amount of bytes to buffer before dropping pkts, in units of 1024, use -1 for AUTO.

reorder_freq.description: How often, out of 1,000,000 packets, should we make a packet out of order.

drop_freq.description: How often, out of 1,000,000 packets, should we purposefully drop a packet.

dup_freq.description: How often, out of 1,000,000 packets, should we purposefully duplicate a packet.

playback_capture.description: ON or OFF, should we play back a WAN capture file?

playback_capture_file.description: Name of the WAN capture file to play back.

playback_loop.description: Should we loop the playback file, YES or NO or NA.

ignore_bandwidth.description: Should we ignore the bandwidth settings from the playback file?  YES, NO, or NA.

ignore_loss.description: Should we ignore the packet-loss settings from the playback file?  YES, NO, or NA.

ignore_latency.description: Should we ignore the latency settings from the playback file?  YES, NO, or NA.

ignore_dup.description: Should we ignore the Duplicate Packet settings from the playback file?  YES, NO, or NA.

jitter_freq.description: How often, out of 1,000,000 packets, should we apply random jitter.

min_drop_amt.description: Minimum amount of packets to drop in a row.  Default is 1.

max_drop_amt.description: Maximum amount of packets to drop in a row.  Default is 1.

min_reorder_amt.description: Minimum amount of packets by which to reorder, Default is 1.

max_reorder_amt.description: Maximum amount of packets by which to reorder, Default is 10.

drop_every_xth_pkt.description: YES to periodically drop every Xth pkt, NO to drop packets randomly.

dup_every_xth_pkt.description: YES to periodically duplicate every Xth pkt, NO to duplicate packets randomly.

reorder_every_xth_pkt.description: YES to periodically reorder every Xth pkt, NO to reorder packets randomly.

test_mgr.description: The name of the Test-Manager this WanPath is to use.  Leave blank for no restrictions.

max_lateness.description: Maximum amount of un-intentional delay before pkt is dropped.  Default is AUTO

follow_binomial.description: YES to have ok/drop burst lengths follow a binomial distribution.

tools.name: apply_vr_cfg
tools.description: Apply all of the virtual routing settings for this Resource. This causes the routing tables to be created and configured properly for the specified configuration. This command should be run after making one or more changes to the virtual routers or virtual router connections. Please note that running this command when there are lots of virtual routers configured can take a long time. Check the status of the Card for percentage complete. Also, while this process is running, you will not be able to configure ports or virtual-router configuration.

The results will be associated with the VOIP endpoint and may be displayed with the show_pesq command

tools.name: reset_port
tools.description: This command will cause the driver on the selected ports to reset the driver (admin down, admin up). It will also re-initialize all of the routing information for that interface. This command will disrupt traffic, but it can be useful if the port locks up or if you wish to restart higher level services (such as dhcp and supplicant for wifi). See the user-guide section on setting up IP addresses and routing for more information. Do not override the default of YES for reset_ospf unless you are certain that is the right thing to do. The pre_ifdown field controls portal login/logout activity and may not actually cause the lower-level driver information to be reset. If left blank or set to NA , then the port will be reset as described above (and any existing ifdown/up scripts will be aborted), and the portal logout script will not be called. Basic options are as follows:

port.description: Port number to reset, or ALL.

reset_ospf.description: If set to 'NO' or 'NA', then OSPF will not be updated.  Otherwise, it will be updated.

pre_ifdown.description: See above.  Leave blank or use NA if unsure.

metadata.syntax: reset_port  shelf resource port reset_ospf pre_ifdown

tools.name: set_arm_info
tools.description: Set Armageddon Endpoint configuration. You may enter AUTO for any value that you wish LANforge to calculate for you or set to defaults. Note that randomizing many of these values will mean packets may not be received on the receiving port due to routing or switching issues. If multi_pkts is set to a value greater than 1, that number of identical packets will be sent before creating a new packet. This can significantly increase performance, but at the cost of not having as much accuracy when calculating latency values. It will also cause the 'duplicate packet' to increment. Armageddon-flags are as follows:

name.description: Name of the Endpoint we are setting.

min_pkt_size.description: Minimum packet size, including all Ethernet headers (but not CRC).

max_pkt_size.description: Maximum packet size, including all Ethernet headers (but not CRC).

ip_src_min.description: Minimum source IP address to use.

ip_src_max.description: Maximum source IP address to use.

ip_dst_min.description: Minimum destination IP address to use.

ip_dst_max.description: Maximum destination IP address to use.

src_mac_count.description: How many source MACs to iterate through.

dst_mac_count.description: How many destination MACs to iterate through.

multi_pkts.description: The number of identical packets to send before creating a new one.

pkts_to_send.description: The number of packets to send.  Set to zero for infinite.

arm_flags.description: Armageddon-related flags, see above for details.

burst.description: Burst amount, can significantly improve throughput with some modern drivers, similar to 'multi_pkts', and uses the 'xmit_more' linux skb option.

metadata.syntax: set_arm_info  name min_pkt_size max_pkt_size udp_src_min udp_src_max udp_dst_min udp_dst_max ip_src_min ip_src_max ip_dst_min ip_dst_max src_mac_count dst_mac_count src_mac dst_mac multi_pkts pkts_to_send arm_flags burst

tools.description: Set RF Noise-generator (RADAR) config. Radar-Type: FCC0: 0 # Uses pulse-width, pulse-interval, pulse-count FCC1: 1 # Uses pulse-width, pulse-interval, pulse-count FCC2: 2 # Uses pulse-width, pulse-interval, pulse-count FCC3: 3 # Uses pulse-width, pulse-interval, pulse-count FCC4: 4 # Uses pulse-width, pulse-interval, pulse-count FCC5: 5 # Uses num-bursts, trials-center, trials-low, trials-high, uut-channel, freq-modulation FCC5B: 6 # Uses burst-offset, pulse-width, chirp-freq-modulation, prf-1, prf-2, prf-3, pulse-count, uut-channel, carrier-freq FCC6 7 # num-bursts (configured with 'pulse_count' field) ETSI1: 8 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1 ETSI2: 9 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1 ETSI3: 10 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1 ETSI4: 11 # Uses pulse-width, prf ETSI5: 12 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, prf-2, prf-3 ETSI6: 13 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, prf-2, prf-3 W53PULSE: 14 # Uses pulse-width, prf, number-of-pulses W53CHIRP: 15 # Uses pulse-width, pri, long-pulse, chirp-width, prf, num-continuous-pairs, center-freq GENERIC: 16 # Uses pulse-width, pulse-interval, pulse-count OFDM: 17 # Modulated wifi signal. Uses duration, header-modulation, payload-modulation, on-t1, off-t1, on-t2, off-t2, on-t3, off-t3 PULSE_DETECT: 18 # Launch rf analyzer, uses freq, trigger_dbm W56PULSE: 19 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, pulse-count KOREA1: 20 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, pulse-count KOREA2: 21 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, pulse-count KOREA3: 22 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, pulse-count KOREA4: 23 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, pulse-count CHINA0: 24 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1 CHINA1: 25 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1 CHINA2: 26 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1 CHINA3: 27 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1 CHINA4: 28 # Modulated radar test signal. Uses pulse-wdith, prf-1 CHINA5: 29 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, prf-2, prf-3 CHINA6: 30 # Uses pulse-width, prf-1, prf-2, prf-3

rfgen_flags_mask.description: Mask of what flags to set, see above.

pulse_width_us.description: Requested pulse width, units are in micro-seconds.  Fractional units (0.5) accepted.

pulse_count.description: Number of pulses (0-255).  Continuous pairs of pulses for W53.

sweep_time_ms.description: Time interval between pulse groups in miliseconds

gain.description: Main TX/RX Amp, 0 or 14 (dB), default is 14

radar_type.description: FCC, ETSI and other RF noise patterns.

burst_offset.description: FCC5B burst offset in usec.  Blank-time for W53-Chirp.

long_pulse_width_us.description: Requested long pulse width for W53 chirp, units are in micro-seconds.

ofdm_t1_on.description: OFDM time-period one on duration in usec

ofdm_t1_off.description: OFDM time-period one off duration in usec

ofdm_t2_on.description: OFDM time-period two on duration in usec

ofdm_t2_off.description: OFDM time-period two off duration in usec

tools.name: set_wifi_radio
tools.description: Modify a WIFI Radio Interface (such as phy0 or wiphy0 ). This command requires that the designated machine support the LANforge wifi driver for the Atheros brand WIFI NICs. The radio interface holds common configuration for the Virtual WiFi interfaces. NA can be used for any values that you do not wish to modify. NOTE: this command is also used for radios dedicated for radar emulation . For adjusting vAPs, only channel, NSS, and txpower are commonly used. To set any option to default (or un-set value, use DEFAULT . You may have to reboot the system to have the defaults take affect. Mode options are below: Antenna settings determine number of active antennae: Flags are currently defined as: The firmware_cfg flag is not saved in LANforge databases, so if you are reloading databases, you may have to manually re-apply the firmware settings. The config data for the last apply is stored on local disk and used by the driver when it loads on bootup. const_tx : This is only supported on carl9170 adapters with modified firmware. Contact your supplier if you want more info on this feature.

radio.description: Name of the physical radio interface, for example: wiphy0

mode.description: RF Pattern Generator: WiFi mode for radar emulation, see table. Do not use for vAPs.

country.description: Country number for this radio device.

rate.description: No longer used, specify the rate on the virtual station(s) instead.

rts.description: The RTS Threshold for this radio (off, or 1-2347).

txpower.description: The transmit power setting for this radio. (AUTO for system defaults)

mac.description: Used to identify when name cannot be trusted (2.6.34+ kernels).

antenna.description: Antenna configuration:  0 Diversity/All, 1 Fixed-A (1x1), 4 AB (2x2), 7 ABC (3x3), 8 ABCD (4x4), 9 8x8

flags.description: Flags for this interface (see above.)

flags_mask.description: If set, only these flags will be considered.

const_tx.description: RF Pattern Generator: encoded as a single 32-bit integer. See above.

pulse_width.description: RF Pattern generator:  pulse width in usecs.

pulse_interval.description: RF Pattern generator:  interval between pulses in usecs.

peer_count.description: Number of peer objects for this radio.

stations_count.description: Number of stations supported by this radio.

rate_ctrl_count.description: Number of rate-ctrl objects for this radio.

txdesc_count.description: Transmit descriptor count for this radio.

tools.name: set_wifi_extra2
tools.description: This configures WiFi ports with advanced features. Not all combinations are valid. Contact support and/or see wpa_supplicant & hostapd configuration documentation for details. Most values will default to sane values if left blank. To clear a text value, set it to [BLANK] . freq_24 and freq_5 are used to configure a subset of available channels that can be used. See add_venue for syntax definition. For stations, the behaviour is thus: If the parent radio has a VAP, then the configured frequency for the radio will be used. Else if the user has configured freq_24 or freq_5, that will be used. However, if the mode specifies a frequency range (ie /b or /g), then frequencies outside of the selected band will still not be allowed.

tools.name: set_wifi_txo
tools.description: This allows one to configure a VAP or Station wifi device to override the normal rate-control and send Data and QOS Data frames at the exact rates specified. This may only work on certain radios/firmware. It can be used to do packet-error testing and other testing where controlling the TX rate is important. txo_txpower notes: For ath10k wifi-5 radios, this is a power setting, for MTK radios, this is an adjustment from default, where 8 is default, less than 8 is reducing power, and more than 8 is increasing power above default. sgi notes: For HT and VHT, 0 is disable SGI, 1 is enable SGI. For HE-SU, 0: 1xLTF+0.8us, 1: 2xLTF+0.8us, 2: 2xLTF+1.6us, 3: 4xLTF+3.2us, 4: 4xLTF+0.8us Flags are currently defined as:

txo_enable.description: Set to 1 if you wish to enable transmit override, 0 to disable.

txo_txpower.description: Configure TX power in db.  Use 255 for system defaults. See notes above.

txo_pream.description: Select rate preamble: 0 == OFDM, 1 == CCK, 2 == HT, 3 == VHT, 4 == HE_SU, 5 = EHT.

txo_mcs.description: Configure the MCS (0-3 for CCK, 0-7 for OFDM, 0-7 for HT, 0-9 for VHT, 0-11 for HE, 0-13 for EHT

txo_nss.description: Configure number of spatial streams (0 == nss1, 1 == nss2, ...).

txo_retries.description: Configure number of retries. 0 or 1 means no retries).

txo_sgi.description: Guard interval and LTF, see above.

txo_flags_mask.description: Specify which txo_flags should be changed.

metadata.syntax: set_wifi_txo  shelf resource port txo_enable txo_txpower txo_pream txo_mcs txo_nss txo_bw txo_retries txo_sgi txo_flags txo_flags_mask

tools.name: set_wifi_corruptions
tools.description: This lets one configure a station or AP to purposely corrupt, delay, and drop various management frames. To disable a corruption, set it to 0. To have corruption always happen, set to maximum value (1000000). For the delay options, this will effectively delay the response by a random number of miliseconds between the configured min and max. This command is primarily for WiFi stations at this time. For AP devices, see the set_wifi_extra2 command. To specify which packet types are to be affected, set the Corrupt Flags accordingly:

req_flush.description: Set to 1 if you wish to flush changes to kernel now.

ignore_per_mil.description: Per-million:  Station to randomly ignore selected message types by this amount.

ignore_flags.description: Specify packet types to ignore (see flags above).

corrupt_per_mil.description: Per-million:  Station to randomly corrupt selected message types by this amount.

corrupt_flags.description: Specify packet types to corrupt (see flags above).

delay_min.description: miliseconds:  Station to randomly delay processing received messages, min time

delay_max.description: miliseconds:  Station to randomly delay processing received messages, max time

delay_flags.description: Specify packet types to delay (see flags above).

dup_flags.description: Specify packet types to duplicate (see flags above).

dup_per_65535.description: Percentage, represented as x per 65535 of packets we should duplicate.

pmkid.description: PMKID Override. Provide in hex (without '0x'). Up to 16-bytes (32 characters).

metadata.syntax: set_wifi_corruptions  shelf resource port req_flush ignore_per_mil ignore_flags corrupt_per_mil corrupt_flags delay_min delay_max delay_flags dup_flags dup_per_65535 pmkid

tools.name: set_endp_details
tools.description: Modify TCP window sizes. The rcvbuf_size will be passed to: and the sndbuf will be set similarly: See the socket man page: man socket for more detailed information about what this means. conn_timer is used to create TCP connections of short duration. If this is set to some value other than 0xFFFFFFFF , then the connection will be closed and reopened at that duration. Set to a low value for testing firewalls and devices that are interested in connections-per-second. dst_mac is used for custom-ethernet endpoints that are replaying pkts and my want to re-write the DST MAC as we replay.

min_reconn_pause.description: The minimum time between re-connects, in ms.

max_reconn_pause.description: The maximum time between re-connects, in ms.

max_ip_port.description: The maximum IP Port value. (The value for min ip port is set through the add_endp/ip_port parameter.) If greater than min, each connection will use a random value between min and max.

conn_timeout.description: For TCP, the max time in miliseconds to wait for connection to establish.

tools.name: set_endp_tos
tools.description: Set the IP Type of Service (TOS) byte for this Endpoint. Only valid for TCP/IP and UDP/IP based endpoint types. You should consult RFC-791, RFC-1349 and RFC-2474 for ideas of what this value can and should be. RFC 1394 standard TOS settings can be entered by name: You may also instruct the Endpoint to NOT set any TOS with the TOS keyword: DONT-SET . This will make the Endpoint use the kernel defaults. If you have already set the TOS, then you must stop and restart the Endpoint to have the new default values take affect. For Priority , please read the Linux socket man page: man 7 socket

This is good for gracefully finishing the last transaction and allowing all the packets in flight to be received by the receiving end (which continues to function as normal during the quiesce.) Use set_cx_state to actually put the endpoint in quiesce state.

tools.name: set_port
tools.description: This command allows you to modify attributes on an Ethernet port. These options includes the IP address, netmask, gateway address, MAC, MTU, and TX Queue Length. In order for this command to succeed the Endpoints which are using the port must not be running. Endpoints which use IP will be updated automatically with the appropriate information if the port is modified. If you do not wish to modify one or more of the settings, enter 'NA' instead of a real value. For the flags entries, add up as many flags as you wish to set, and enter the sum. For example, if you want to set flag 1, 2, and 8, then enter: 11, or 0xB . When setting the link speed with current_flags , use one of the Fixed flags and don't set auto-negotiate for fixed mode, or set as many of the advert flags as you wish and set auto-negotiate for auto-negotiate mode. Normally, you will advertise everything your resource is capable of. current_flags can be: cmd_flags can be: The interest flags are used to specify which combinations of other parameters to combine. If you specify command.dhcp_ip4 but do not specify interest.dhcp, the command flag will not be applied. These flags are not obvious to combine, so please apply the settings you want into the GUI, and then find the set_port command in the DB/DFLT/ports.db.1.1 file. interest flag values are: flags2 values are: IPv6 Address format is: addr/prefix Scope is implied by the position (first address is global, etc). NOTE: You may create custom dhclient config files if you need more flexibility than the built-in features LANforge supports. dhcp_vendor_id: dhcp_hostname: dhcp_client_id:

shelf.description: Shelf number for the port to be modified. [R]

resource.description: Resource number for the port to be modified.

port.description: Port number for the port to be modified.

ip_addr.description: IP address for the port, or NA.

netmask.description: Netmask which this port should use, or NA.

gateway.description: IP address of the gateway device - used for IP routing, or NA.

cmd_flags.description: Command Flags: See above, or NA.

MTU.description: Maximum Transmit Unit (MTU) for this interface.  Can be blank or NA.

tx_queue_len.description: Transmit Queue Length for this interface.  Can be blank or NA.

alias.description: A user-defined name for this interface.  Can be BLANK or NA.

interest.description: Which things are we really interested in setting.  Can over-ride defaults based on the other arguments.

report_timer.description: How often, in milliseconds, should we poll stats on this interface?

br_aging_time.description: MAC aging time, in seconds, 32-bit number (or peer IP for GRE).

br_max_age.description: How long until STP considers a non-responsive bridge dead.

br_hello_time.description: How often does the bridge send out STP hello packets.

br_forwarding_delay.description: How long to wait until the bridge will start forwarding packets.

br_port_cost.description: STP Port cost for a port (this applies only to NON-BRIDGE interfaces).

current_flags_msk.description: This sets 'interest' for flags 'Enable RADIUS service' and higher. See above, or NA.

dhcp_vendor_id.description: Optional string of up to 63 bytes in length to be passed to the dhclient process. See above.

ipsec_passwd.description: Password for IPSec, for pubkey, use: pubkey:[pem-file-name], for instance: pubkey:station.pem

ipsec_local_id.description: Local Identifier for this IPSec tunnel.

ipsec_remote_id.description: Remote Identifier for this IPSec tunnel.

dhcp_hostname.description: Optional string of up to 63 bytes in length to be passed to the dhclient process.  Option 12, see above.

tools.name: set_voip_info
tools.description: Set various VOIP endpoint related values. Use this to enable behaviour different from the defaults (see add_voip_endp, and set_endp_flag). If the min and max values are different, a random value in that range will be chosen. Any values can be 'NA' and they will be ignored. If min/max_call_duration is less than the length of the wave file multiplied by the number of times to play the wave file, then the max_call_duration will determine the call length. If Min/Max call duration are not the same, a random value between the min and max will be chosen each time a call is started. Otherwise, the call will be determined by the wave file size & repetition. The registration expire timer affects the sip messaging protocol: The default of 300 is fine in most cases. The sound_dev determines which sound device to play the received RTP stream on. Usually /dev/dsp or /dev/audio is the correct value.

name.description: The name of the endpoint we are configuring.

first_call_delay.description: How long to wait before making first call, in seconds.

min_inter_call_gap.description: Minimum time to wait between calls, in seconds.

max_inter_call_gap.description: Maximum time to wait between calls, in seconds.

loop_call_count.description: How many calls to make, zero means infinite.

loop_wavefile_count.description: How many times to play the wave file, zero means infinite.

min_call_duration.description: How long should the call be, in seconds.

max_call_duration.description: How long should the call be, in seconds.

ringing_timer.description: How long (milliseconds) to wait in the ringing state before flagging call as no-answer.

PESQ_server_port.description: LANforge PESQ server port, default is 3998.

PESQ_server_passwd.description: LANforge PESQ server password.  Default is to use no authentication (blank entry).

jitter_buffer_sz.description: The size of the jitter buffer in packets.  Default value is 8.

aq_call_report_count.description: Number of AQ Call Report. Default is 0.

aq_audio_band.description: Audio band for AQ scoring. 0: narrow-band, 1: super-wide-band, 2: full-band.  Default is 0.

tools.name: set_wanpath_filter
tools.description: Set the filter type for the WanPath. If the filter is set to MAC, then it will match based on the source and/or destination MAC address. If IP is chosen, it will match on the source and destination IP addresses and masks. Default behaviour is to match on the IP address. MAC syntax is: 00:11:22:33:44:55 IP Syntax is: a.b.c.d/24 or a.b.c.d/255.255.255.0 PCAP syntax is same as for tcpdump. Use 'man tcpdump' on Linux, or see this page: http://www.tcpdump.org/tcpdump_man.html The 'passive' argument is to allow you to set the pcap filter, but not actually use it (perhaps you are using IP filtering, but we want to remember the pcap filter for later.)

wl_name.description: The name of the WanLink endpoint we are configuring.

wp_name.description: The name of the WanPath we are configuring.

filter_type.description: The filter type, one of: MAC, IP, PCAP.

src_filter.description: The source MAC or IP/Mask.  For PCAP, this is the only filter.

dst_filter.description: The destination MAC or IP/Mask, 'NA' for PCAP.

reverse.description: If you want the logic reversed, use 'ON', otherwise set to 'OFF'

defer_flush.description: Enter 'YES' if you do NOT want this flushed to the remote.

passive.description: Enter 'YES' if you do NOT want to use this filter currently.

metadata.syntax: set_wanpath_filter  wl_name wp_name filter_type src_filter dst_filter reverse defer_flush passive

tools.name: set_wanpath_running

running.description: The state, one of: AS_PARENT, RUNNING, STOPPED.

tools.name: set_wanpath_corruption
tools.description: Set a corruption for a WanPath. Corruptions include random and fixed over-write of a byte in the Ethernet frame, as well as random bit-flips and bit transposes. Up to 6 corruptions are supported per WanLink. If the 'chain' flag is set on a corruption, then if that corruption is chosen to be applied, the next corruption will always be applied. The 'byte' specifies the byte to write into the frame, if OVERWRITE_FIXED flag is chosen. The min and max offset determine the possible position of the byte to be modified. If min is less than max, a random byte between min and max will be modified. The offset is from the beginning of the Ethernet header. The 'rate' specifies how often, per million, the corruption will be applied. This is flat-random distribution. The flags are defined as: The RECALC_CSUMS option will ONLY work if the UDP or TCP packet spans a single Ethernet frame. Only one of the first 4 bits should be selected. Add flag values together to set multiple flags.

name.description: WanLink name

flags.description: The flags for this corruption.

byte.description: The byte to use for OVERWRITE_FIXED (or NA).

min_offset.description: The minimum offset from start of Ethernet packet for the byte to be modified.

max_offset.description: The maximum offset from start of Ethernet packet for the byte to be modified.

rate.description: Specifies how often, per million, this corruption should be applied.

metadata.syntax: set_wanpath_corruption  name path index flags byte min_offset max_offset rate

tools.name: set_wanlink_info
tools.description: Set the WanLink information for an endpoint. You can set the Latency, MaxJitter, and reorder characteristics here. Special attention should be paid to extra_buffer. This setting should be zero, or a small number, if you are doing latency-sensitive testing. Use -1 if you want LANforge to automatically configure a proper extra_buffer size based on your maximum bandwidth. The server will add the extra_buffer size to a calculated buffer size based on the maximum jitter and latency specified in the WanLink endpoint. If you wish to drop bursts of packets, then set the min_drop_amt and max_drop_amt. When LANforge determines that a packet drop should occur (based on the drop_freq), then it will also pick a random value between the min and max drop_amt and drop that many packets in a row. The value of all attributes other than the name can be 'NA', which means do not change the current value.

name.description: The name of the endpoint we are configuring.

speed.description: The maximum speed of traffic this endpoint will accept (bps).

latency.description: The base latency added to all packets, in milliseconds (or add 'us' suffix for microseconds

max_jitter.description: The maximum jitter, in milliseconds (or ad 'us' suffix for microseconds)

reorder_freq.description: How often, out of 1,000,000 packets, should we make a packet out of order.

extra_buffer.description: The extra amount of bytes to buffer before dropping pkts, in units of 1024.  Use -1 for AUTO.

drop_freq.description: How often, out of 1,000,000 packets, should we purposefully drop a packet.

dup_freq.description: How often, out of 1,000,000 packets, should we purposefully duplicate a packet.

playback_capture_file.description: Name of the WAN capture file to play back.

jitter_freq.description: How often, out of 1,000,000 packets, should we apply jitter.

min_drop_amt.description: Minimum amount of packets to drop in a row.  Default is 1.

max_drop_amt.description: Maximum amount of packets to drop in a row.  Default is 1.

min_reorder_amt.description: Minimum amount of packets by which to reorder, Default is 1.

max_reorder_amt.description: Maximum amount of packets by which to reorder, Default is 10.

max_lateness.description: Maximum amount of un-intentional delay before pkt is dropped.  Default is AUTO

metadata.syntax: set_wanlink_info  name speed latency max_jitter reorder_freq extra_buffer drop_freq dup_freq playback_capture_file jitter_freq min_drop_amt max_drop_amt min_reorder_amt max_reorder_amt max_lateness

The generated files for both WanLink endpoints can then be played back across a network using the LANforge playback features. The capture will start and stop with the endpoint, and it will write over any existing file so be careful. To mitigate the risk, if the path is absolute, it must start with /tmp or /home/lanforge. To effectively store files elsewhere, you can set up soft-links to directories within one of these directory trees. Capture Options:

name.description: The name of the endpoint we are configuring.

capture.description: Should we capture or not? ON or OFF.

directory.description: The directory name in which packet capture files will be written.

tools.name: set_wl_corruption
tools.description: Set a corruption for WanLink. Corruptions include random and fixed over-write of a byte in the Ethernet frame, as well as random bit-flips and bit transposes. Specific rules apply: Up to 6 corruptions are supported per WanLink. If the chain flag is set on a corruption, then if that corruption is chosen to be applied, the next corruption will always be applied. If OVERWRITE_FIXED flag is chosen, the 'byte' specifies the byte to write into the frame. The min_offset and max_offset determine the possible position of the byte to be modified. If min is less than max, a random byte between min and max will be modified. The offset is from the beginning of the Ethernet header. The rate specifies how often, per million, the corruption will be applied. This is flat-random distribution. The flags are defined as: The RECALC_CSUMS option will ONLY work if the UDP or TCP packet spans a single Ethernet frame. Only one of the first 4 bits should be selected. Add flag values together to set multiple flags.

name.description: WanLink name

flags.description: The flags for this corruption.

byte.description: The byte to use for OVERWRITE_FIXED (or NA).

min_offset.description: The minimum offset from start of Ethernet packet for the byte to be modified.

max_offset.description: The maximum offset from start of Ethernet packet for the byte to be modified.

rate.description: Specifies how often, per million, this corruption should be applied.

metadata.syntax: set_wl_corruption  name index flags byte min_offset max_offset rate

tools.name: set_wl_qdisc
tools.description: Set a Queuing Discipline on the WanLink. For WRR you must specify the weights (and in doing so, the number of queues): The packet priority will be mapped directly onto the queues. If the packet priority cannot be queried from the OS, the 3 IP ToS bits will be used for priority, so we recommend 7 queues for WRR QDiscs. Others queuing disciplines may be added in the future.

name.description: WanLink name

tools.name: set_endp_file
tools.description: Set the file name for an endpoint. In the future, this may affect various endpoint types differently, but for now it is only used to set the capture file that a Custom Ethernet endpoint can 'play back'. To use this feature, first use a WanLink connection to capture packets flowing across a network. The WanLink connections can be configured to save all incoming packets to a file. The Customer Ethernet connection can then be configured with one of the capture files associated with each endpoint. During playback, each endpoint will play back the packet stream as it arrived, inserting pauses between the packets, and ensuring that packets are placed on the wire in the same order that they were received. file can be blank or NA if you wish to only turn playback on or off. Playback options:

name.description: The name of the endpoint we are configuring.

file.description: The file name to read the playback packets from.

tools.description: Show one/all ports for one/all resources in one/all shelves. Probe-Flags:

probe_flags.description: See above, add them together for multiple probings.  Leave blank if you want stats only.

tools.name: show_mlo_link
tools.description: Show one/all MLO Links for one/all ports for one/all resources in one/all shelves.

tools.name: show_channel_groups
tools.description: Show one/all ChannelGroups for one/all resources in one/all shelves. An empty specifier will be treated as 'all'.

tools.name: show_spans
tools.description: Show one/all Spans for one/all resources in one/all shelves. An empty specifier will be treated as 'all'.

tools.name: show_ppp_links
tools.description: Show one/all PPP Links for one/all resources in one/all shelves. An empty specifier will be treated as 'all'.

link_num.description: Ppp-Link number of the span, or 'all'.

tools.name: show_tm
tools.description: Show one or all test managers.

test_mgr.description: Can be name of test manager, or 'all'.

tools.name: show_group
tools.description: Show one or all Connection Groups.

group.description: Can be name of connection group.  Use 'all' or leave blank for all groups.

tools.name: show_venue
tools.description: Show one or more venues

venu_id.description: Number to uniquely identify this venue on this resource, or 'ALL'

tools.name: show_wps

tools.name: shutdown
tools.description: Restart the LANforge Manager server. Restarting the manager will cause interruption to all of the Resource processes as well. If you want to restart all LANforge processes on the Manager machine, enter 'YES' for the third argument (and probably 'NA' for the second)

really.description: Must be 'YES' for command to really work.

chdir.description: Directory to cd to before dying.  Only useful when using gprof to debug, or 'NA' to ignore.

serverctl.description: Enter 'YES' to do a ./serverctl.bash restart to restart all LANforge processes.

tools.name: shutdown_resource
tools.description: This will restart the LANforge processes on the resource specified. This will cause all tests that are utilizing that resource to be destroyed. Depending on how the system is set up, the remote resource will probably be restarted in about 5 seconds.

tools.name: shutdown_os
tools.description: This will bring down the Operating System on the resource specified, including all processes running on it. Only a power-cycle will bring it back up again. This command should be used before powering down the LANforge resources. Wait about 1 minute before shutting off the power to allow the OS to bring itself down gracefully. See also: reboot_OS

tools.name: sniff_port
tools.description: This will attempt to launch the Wireshark program on the specified port's machine and display Wireshark to the specified X server. Wireshark will be tried first, but if it is not found, Ethereal will be attempted. You must be running X, and have allowed other machines to connect to your X server. If you do not specify the DISPLAY , LANforge will attempt to guess it based on your connecting IP address. For PCs, you can use the exceed program from Hummingbird software. To enable X access on Unix/Linux, run this command: This can open your machine to security threats, so read up on xhost before you run this command on a mission critical machine not protected by a good firewall! Port: You may also specify multiple interfaces to sniff concurrently using syntax: port1,port2,port3 Flags are defined as follows. Learn more about Wireshark program . For questions specific to LANforge, you should contact Candela Technologies.

port.description: The port we are trying to run the packet sniffer on.  See above.

display.description: The DISPLAY option, for example: 192.168.1.5:0.0.  Will guess if left blank.

flags.description: Flags that control how the sniffing is done.

outfile.description: Optional file location for saving a capture.

duration.description: Duration for doing a capture (in seconds).  Default is 5 minutes for dumpcap/tshark, and forever for wireshark

snaplen.description: Amount of each packet to store.  Default is to store all of it.

metadata.syntax: sniff_port  shelf resource port display flags outfile duration snaplen filter

tools.name: tail
tools.description: Deal with 'tailing' a file. This is usually going to be a log file. This displays a GUI popup. This does not stream text to JSON. If you need to see the end of a log file, use logfile

shelf.description: Shelf that holds the resource that holds the file. [R]

key.description: File-name that we should be tailing.

metadata.syntax: tail  shelf resource cmd key message

tools.name: tm_register
tools.description: When a client is registered with a test manager, the manager will send the client reports at specified intervals (see set_tm_rpt).

test_mgr.description: Name of test manager (can be all.)

client_name.description: Name of client to be registered. (dflt is current client)

tools.name: tm_unregister
tools.description: The client will receive no more un-requested reports from the test manager(s).

test_mgr.description: Name of test manager (can be all.)

client_name.description: Name of client to be un-registered. (dflt is current client)

tools.name: version
tools.description: Print out the version of the LANforge server.

tools.name: wiser_reset
tools.description: This command will reset the WISER library on the specified machine. This is only useful when running with the Telcordia WISER module.

tools.name: who
tools.description: Show who is currently logged into the system.

tools.name: wifi_event
tools.description: This is used internally by LANforge to listen for WiFi events.

device.description: Interface or PHY in most cases.

msg.description: Entire event in human readable form.

tools.name: wifi_cli_cmd
tools.description: LANforge WiFi station interfaces are controlled by the wpa_supplicant process, which can be directly manipulated with the wpa_cli command. For normal LANforge use, users will not need to deal directly with wpa_supplicant or wpa_cli. For more advanced features, such as roaming, users will need to use wpa_cli commands directly. This LANforge API makes that easier to accomplish. Example: NOTE: These commands will be queued if the interface is phantom, but otherwise the commands will be sent to the wpa_cli command immediately. This can collide with automated LANforge actions such as automatically re-associating and interface that was dropped by the AP. Any configuration changes made by this method will not be saved through restarts of LANforge or even through network interface resets. If the port is a VAP, then the command will be passed to the hostapd_cli process in a similar manner.

port.description: Name of the WiFi station or AP interface to which this command will be directed.

wpa_cli_cmd.description: Command to pass to wpa_cli or hostap_cli.  This must be single-quoted.

tools.name: xorpsh
tools.description: Connect to a Virtual Router's xorpsh shell. The cmd parameter value display determines what X11 screen the terminal will appear on. (You must be running X windows on the target DISPLAY system.) For display mode, the argument is the display address and screen number found in typical DISPLAY environment variables for this instance of xorpsh. Example: DISPLAY=localhost:10.0 For 'run_cmd' mode, it is the command (in single quotes) to pass to the xorpsh process.

cmd.description: Determines action, current commands:  display, run_cmd

metadata.syntax: xorpsh  shelf resource router cmd arg
